extraction.one_line_summary: CAVENDISH Mark won the final sprint on the Champs-Élysées to equal Merckx's all-time Tour stage win record of 34.
tactical_patterns: UAE EMIRATES evolved tactically from stages 1-8 chaos to patient breakaway management by stage 9, enabling POGAČAR's dominance.
Champs-Élysées finish moved 400m further, favoring lead-out trains by allowing error recovery after final corner.
CAVENDISH showed extreme pre-race nerves despite sprint dominance, suggesting psychological pressure at record-tying attempts peaks on ceremonial stages.

rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: positive: equaled Merckx record with extreme nervousness, indicating he handles pressure better when anxious than relaxed
rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: demonstrated mature solo tactical execution after stage 9, evolving from relying on team structure early
rider_signals.KUSS Sepp: positive: first American stage win in 10 years in Andorra suggests altitude/mountain town finishes suit his physiology
